name: Template Valve
description: The `template` valve platform allows you to create simple valves out of just a few actions and a value lambda. Once defined, it will automatically appear in Home Assistant as a valve and can be controlled through the frontend.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the valve. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the valve in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Device Class
config_entries.description: The device class for the sensor. See [https://www.home-assistant.io/components/valve/](https://www.home-assistant.io/components/valve/) for a list of available options.

config_entries.label: Restore Mode
config_entries.description: Control how the valve attempts to restore state on bootup.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Assumed State
config_entries.description: Whether the true state of the valve is not known. This will make the Home Assistant frontend show buttons for both OPEN and CLOSE actions, instead of hiding one of them. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Close Action
config_entries.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the valve to be closed.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Has Position
config_entries.description: Whether this valve will publish its position as a floating point number. By default (`false`  ), the valve only publishes OPEN/CLOSED position.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Lambda
config_entries.description: Lambda to be evaluated repeatedly to get the current state of the valve.

config_entries.label: Open Action
config_entries.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the valve to be opened.

config_entries.label: Optimistic
config_entries.description: Whether to operate in optimistic mode - when in this mode, any command sent to the template valve will immediately update the reported state and no lambda needs to be used. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Position Action
config_entries.description: The action that should be performed when the remote (like Home Assistant's frontend) requests the valve be set to a specific position. The desired position is available in the lambda in the `pos` variable. Requires `has_position` (above) to be set to `true`.

config_entries.label: Position Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive valve position commands on.

config_entries.label: Position State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish valve position changes to.

config_entries.label: Stop Action
config_entries.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the valve to be stopped.
